topic.what: Zinc-L-carnosine (polaprezinc) is a chelated compound of zinc and L-carnosine that exerts gastroprotective, antioxidant, and anti-ulcer effects in the gastric mucosa.
topic.how: In-vivo animal studies demonstrating that polaprezinc adheres preferentially to ulcer sites in the stomach, enabling sustained local zinc release that supports mucosal healing and suppresses oxidative stress.
topic.why_matters: Polaprezinc became widely prescribed in Japan as a mucosal-protective anti-ulcer agent; its site-specific adhesion and sustained zinc delivery distinguish it mechanistically from conventional zinc salts or antacids.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Preferential binding of polaprezinc to ulcerated vs. healthy gastric tissue
characteristics.vs_baseline: Greater adhesion at ulcer sites than intact mucosa
characteristics.evidence: In-vivo animal ulcer model showing compound localisation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Suppression of reactive oxygen species in gastric tissue
characteristics.vs_baseline: Superior to zinc chloride or L-carnosine alone
characteristics.evidence: Biochemical assays in animal stomach tissue
characteristics.context: Chelated form required for optimal activity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in ulcer area following treatment
characteristics.context: Multiple animal ulcer models (acetic acid, indomethacin)

good_for: Gastroprotection and mucosal healing in ulcer disease
Antioxidant support in gastric inflammatory conditions
Basis for clinical use of polaprezinc as adjunct or monotherapy in Japan
not_for: Direct human RCT evidence (this paper is animal/in-vitro)
requires: Chelated zinc-carnosine form for site-specific adhesion
Acidic gastric environment for optimal adhesion behaviour

novelty: First comprehensive characterisation of polaprezinc's dual gastroprotective mechanism: site-specific mucosal adhesion plus sustained zinc release
Demonstrated that the chelated form is necessary for antioxidant superiority over individual components
Established foundational pharmacology underpinning widespread clinical prescription of polaprezinc in Japan
open_problems: Optimal dosing and duration in human clinical populations
Mechanistic interaction between polaprezinc and H. pylori virulence factors
Long-term zinc accumulation safety with chronic mucosal dosing
